relatedness_reasoning: Both files concern account risk and compliance actions; inbound arrears_flags triggers outbound account freeze/block decisions. Risk_and_compliance is both consumer of inbound and provider of outbound, forming a logical pipeline step where arrears severity and risk category drive freeze actions.

mappings.reasoning: System-generated unique identifier for message exchange; no source in inbound schema.
Both are ISO8601 timestamps; outbound timestamp likely derived from or correlated with inbound flag_date when freeze action is initiated.
Inbound metadata.destination_department is 'Risk_and_Compliance', which matches outbound source enum value 'risk_and_compliance'.
Outbound destination 'accounts' is a routing/system configuration value not present in inbound schema.
Message type 'account_freeze_or_block' is a classification of the outbound message itself, not derived from inbound data.
Inbound flag_severity enum includes 'HIGH' and 'CRITICAL' which map to outbound priority 'high' for urgent freeze actions.
When compliance_action_required is true and risk_category indicates DEFAULT_RISK, freeze action is triggered; however mapping is indirect.
Inbound account_id (format=account_number) maps to outbound accountNumber; format conversion to IBAN may occur downstream.
Inbound customer_name directly corresponds to outbound accountHolder name field.
Inbound product_type (Mortgage, Personal Loan, Credit Card) differs from outbound accountType enum (Current Account); weak semantic connection.
Outbound currency 'GBP' is a system/branch configuration value not present in inbound schema.
Outbound branch 'London City' is a location configuration value not derivable from inbound arrears_flags data.
Freeze type 'full' is a policy/decision value not present in inbound schema; determined by compliance rules, not inbound data.
Inbound risk_category (DEFAULT_RISK, WATCH_LIST, EARLY_WARNING) could trigger freeze, but outbound reason enum 'suspected_fraud' does not directly match arrears-based risk categories.
Inbound notes field provides free-text commentary that could populate outbound description narrative.
Name of person initiating freeze is a runtime/operational value not present in inbound schema.
Outbound department 'Fraud Investigation Unit' is a specific organizational unit not derivable from inbound arrears_flags data.
Freeze effective date likely correlates with or is derived from inbound flag_date timestamp.
Transaction restriction policies are compliance/operational decisions not derivable from inbound arrears data.

Inbound flag_severity (MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL) could map to escalation levels, but mapping is indirect and not explicit.
Inbound compliance_action_required boolean could indicate whether formal approval is needed for freeze action.
Approval deadline is a policy/operational value not present in inbound schema.
Assigned compliance officer is a runtime/organizational assignment not present in inbound schema.
